Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Lets break down the process Steps 1 Claim Registration CR The claim is logged into the system 2 Document Verification DV Customers documents policies identity proofs etc are verified 3 Fraud Check FC The claim is examined by a special unit to ensure theres no fraudulent activity 4 Claim Assessment CA The claim is assessed by an adjuster who decides the compensation amount 5 Approval AP The claim must be approved by a senior manager if the amount surpasses a certain threshold 6 Payment P The approved claim is paid out to the customer 7 Notification N The customer is notified about the claim resolution and payment details 8 Closure CL The claim is closed and archived in the system Additionally there are two possible loops Between DV and RDS if documents are incomplete after Document Verification DV a re submission of documents step occurs Re Document Submission RDS before the process continues Similarly if the Fraud Check FC raises doubts an additional In depth Investigation II is performed This can happen multiple times before proceeding to Claim Assessment CA

Name: Abductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Approval AP only if amount surpasses threshold but note the description says must be approved by a senior manager if the amount surpasses a certain threshold However the process doesnt specify what happens if it doesnt surpass We assume that the approval step is conditional and might be skipped in some cases But the description does not explicitly say that the process can skip the Approval step Since the process description is fixed we assume that the Approval step is always performed Therefore we will model the Approval step as a mandatory transition

Name: Inductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Lets list the activities transitions we need CR Claim Registration DV Document Verification FC Fraud Check CA Claim Assessment AP Approval P Payment N Notification CL Closure RDS Re Document Submission II In depth Investigation Well create places between these transitions to model the state We note that the initial marking should have a token in a start place say p_start and the final marking should have a token in a place that represents the closed claim say p_end

Name: Hypothesis Generation - NE
Text: Lets design the Petri net We can think of the process as a sequence with two loops Well create the following places states p_start initial state token in p_start means a new claim is filed p_after_CR after CR before DV p_after_DV after DV before either FC or if documents are incomplete RDS p_after_FC after FC before either CA or if doubts II p_after_II after II back to FC p_after_CA after CA before AP p_after_AP after AP before P p_after_P after P before N p_after_N after N before CL p_end after CL the claim is closed Also we need places for the loops For the DV RDS loop We have a place after DV that can lead to either FC or RDS after DV if documents are incomplete then we have RDS Then after RDS we go back to DV So we can model p_after_DV FC if documents are complete p_after_DV RDS if incomplete Then after RDS we go back to DV So we need a place after RDS that leads to DV Similarly for the FC II loop p_after_FC CA if no doubts p_after_FC II if doubts Then after II we go back to FC So we need a place after II that leads to FC

Name: Deductive Reasoning - NE
Text: We are going to create the following arcs From p_start to t_CR initial token in p_start t_CR consumes it and produces in p_after_CR Then we have a sequence for the main path and the loops Lets draw the net p_start t_CR p_after_CR p_after_CR t_DV p_after_DV From p_after_DV we have two choices p_after_DV t_FC p_after_FC p_after_DV t_RDS p_after_RDS Then p_after_RDS t_DV p_after_DV this creates a loop Similarly from p_after_FC we have two choices p_after_FC t_CA p_after_CA p_after_FC t_II p_after_II Then p_after_II t_FC p_after_FC this creates a loop Then continue the main sequence p_after_CA t_AP p_after_AP p_after_AP t_P p_after_P p_after_P t_N p_after_N p_after_N t_CL p_end And the final marking is a token in p_end

Name: Validation - IND
Text: We must note that the loops are not forced The Petri net allows both paths However note that the description says that the in depth investigation can happen multiple times So we are modeling a loop that can be repeated any number of times

Name: Heuristic Reasoning - NE
Text: Now lets code this in Python using pm4py Steps in code 1 Create a PetriNet object 2 Create places and transitions 3 Add them to the net 4 Create arcs according to the above structure 5 Create the initial marking with a token in p_start and the final marking with a token in p_end Lets code accordingly
